title: Owned Inspection, Ordering, Release, and Collection Integrity · Go Semantics Case: Pokedex Go Owned Lookup
summary: Inspect silently falls back to the network, list order changes with map iteration, release deletes before confirmation, and aliases let cached updates rewrite owned records. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go owned lookup increment in offline inspectable and reversible collection interface. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Distinguish owned inspection from remote lookup and return a clear not-owned result without hidden traffic.
Prove success, miss, duplicate, lookup failure, cancellation, seeded boundary, and unchanged-collection failure cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
